# VER-QUIC-API-0001 - Incursa.Quic Public API Surface Verification

## Scope

This verification slice covers the connection-, listener-, first client-entry, narrow stream-entry, supported write/completion plus narrow reset/stop-sending abort pair, supported combined `Abort(Both, ...)` composition, supported stream-capacity callback, and shared support-marker consumer public API surface, the close/dispose/error projection visible through that surface, the server-side listener validation and accept semantics, the supported positive client/server loopback establishment boundary, the supported positive stream open/accept boundary on that same active loopback path, the supported write/completion plus reset/stop-sending abort boundary on that same active loopback path, the supported combined abort boundary on that same active loopback path, the supported stream-capacity callback boundary on that same path, the shared runtime capability marker semantics, the pending client-connect validation/cancellation/terminal semantics, and the internal-only boundary. It proves that only the approved facade types are public, that `QuicStreamType` has the direction-only shape, that `QuicConnection.IsSupported` and `QuicListener.IsSupported` are the same public capability marker over the same runtime prerequisites, that `QuicListener` and `QuicListenerOptions` honor validation, cancellation, and disposal, that `QuicClientConnectionOptions` and `QuicConnection.ConnectAsync(...)` expose only the approved shape, that `ConnectAsync(...)`, `AcceptInboundStreamAsync(...)`, `OpenOutboundStreamAsync(...)`, and the supported `QuicStream` write/completion plus narrow abort pair return real `QuicConnection` and `QuicStream` facades only after the supported boundaries are reached, that `Abort(Read, ...)`, `Abort(Write, ...)`, and `Abort(Both, ...)` are supported on the approved loopback path, that the supported stream-capacity callback subset reports the initial peer-advertised outbound stream-open increment, later real `MAX_STREAMS` growth, and real peer stream-close-driven capacity release, and that the listed helper/runtime seam types remain internal. It does not attempt to prove full TLS-auth parity, broad stream-management completeness, wire behavior beyond the active loopback path, or performance.

- REQ-QUIC-API-0001

- REQ-QUIC-API-0011

## Verification Method

Execution, inspection, and API-diff evidence from the public API analyzer, plus targeted surface tests derived from the Microsoft public-surface behavior baseline and the existing connection/runtime seams.

## Preconditions

- The approved SPEC-QUIC-API and ARC-QUIC-API-0001 artifacts exist.
- The Incursa.Quic project builds with public API analyzer support enabled.
- The consumer facade types are available for inspection or promotion.
- The Microsoft reference tests used as behavioral evidence have been reviewed and mapped to the refined surface expectations.

## Procedure or Approach

- Compare src/Incursa.Quic/PublicAPI.Unshipped.txt and any future API approval diffs against the approved type list.
- Run compile-time or reflection checks that the approved public types and members are present.
- Run negative access checks that the helper and runtime seam types cannot be referenced from outside the assembly.
- Add or review focused consumer-behavior tests for `QuicStreamType` shape, `QuicConnection.IsSupported`, `QuicListener.IsSupported`, close/dispose/error mapping, read-side and write-side terminal behavior, listener validation, accept cancellation/disposal, positive client/server loopback establishment, supported stream open/accept on the active loopback path, supported write/completion plus `Abort(Read, ...)` / `Abort(Write, ...)` on that path, supported `Abort(Both, ...)` loopback behavior, supported initial stream-capacity callback invocation, client option/connect validation, pending-connect cancellation/disposal, unsupported TLS rejection, and facade-to-runtime/host mapping.
- Review the design note, spec, and the reference Microsoft test evidence together to confirm no extra public abstractions were introduced.

## Expected Result

The public API matches the approved connection-, listener-, client-entry, narrow stream-entry, supported write/completion plus narrow reset/stop-sending abort pair, supported combined `Abort(Both, ...)` composition, supported stream-capacity callback, and shared support-marker consumer surface, the close/dispose/error projection is visible, `QuicStreamType` has the approved direction-only shape, the listener validation and accept semantics are honest, the supported positive loopback establishment boundary is real, the supported positive stream open/accept boundary is real, the supported write/completion plus reset/stop-sending abort boundary is real, `QuicConnection.IsSupported` and `QuicListener.IsSupported` return the same shared runtime capability value, combined `Abort(Both, ...)` is supported on the approved bidirectional loopback path, the supported stream-capacity callback boundary is real for the initial delta, later real `MAX_STREAMS` growth, and real peer stream-close-driven release, pending client connect is honest about cancellation and unsupported TLS settings, and the helper/runtime seam types remain internal.

## Status

Execution evidence now covers the supported positive client/server loopback boundary, the listener/server slice, the connected-session facade, the supported positive stream open/accept boundary on the active loopback path, the supported write/completion plus narrow reset/stop-sending abort pair and supported combined abort composition on that same path, the supported stream-capacity callback boundary on that same path, and the shared support-marker slice. The proof target includes client option/connect validation, unsupported TLS rejection, support-marker determinism, terminal cancellation/disposal behavior, and the narrow stream-entry, write/completion, abort-pair, combined-abort, plus stream-capacity boundaries; full TLS-auth parity and broader stream-management remain out of scope.

## Evidence Notes

The current proof includes build output and focused requirement-home tests for `REQ-QUIC-API-0001`, `REQ-QUIC-API-0002`, `REQ-QUIC-API-0003`, `REQ-QUIC-API-0004`, `REQ-QUIC-API-0005`, `REQ-QUIC-API-0006`, `REQ-QUIC-API-0008`, `REQ-QUIC-API-0009`, `REQ-QUIC-API-0010`, and `REQ-QUIC-API-0011`, including the supported positive loopback establishment, stream-entry, supported write/completion plus reset/stop-sending abort pair, supported combined `Abort(Both, ...)` composition, shared support-marker checks, and stream-capacity callback tests covering the initial delta, later growth, and real close-driven release. The remaining evidence addendum work is now focused on broader TLS/auth parity and broader stream-management follow-ons.
